user_query:
I'm a professional samba dancer and I need a small, reliable weekly job that keeps my practice playlist fresh with openly licensed samba percussion tracks. Please implement a one-shot runner script at scripts/fetch_playlist.py and provide a cron snippet so I can schedule it weekly.

Inputs (provided in the workspace):
- input/preferences.yaml: contains schedule and selection preferences.
- input/schools.csv: my samba schools and aliases to use as additional keywords.

On each run, the script should:
1) Read input/preferences.yaml and input/schools.csv, and compute run_date as the local date in YYYY-MM-DD used for output paths.
2) Discover candidate audio using only official public APIs (no HTML scraping, no login) from:
   - commons.wikimedia.org (Wikimedia Commons)
   - archive.org (Internet Archive)
   Use the keywords from preferences.yaml and also the school names and aliases from schools.csv to search for samba-related audio. Extract structured fields from the API responses: source, identifier (e.g., Commons file title or Internet Archive identifier), title, direct download URL, MIME type, file size in bytes, duration in seconds (if available), and license.
3) Filter, sort, and rank:
   - Keep only items with MIME type starting with audio/ and a common audio extension (e.g., .mp3, .ogg, .wav).
   - Keep only items whose license is in license_allowlist from preferences.yaml.
   - Keep only items with duration between min_duration_sec and max_duration_sec (skip if duration is unknown).
   - Deduplicate by (source, identifier).
   - Rank by duration (descending), then by file_size_bytes (descending) as a tie-breaker. Select up to max_items.
   - For each selected item, compute matched_keyword (the first keyword that matched) and matched_school (the first school or alias from schools.csv that appears in the title or metadata), or empty if none.
4) Download the selected audio files into out/audio/<run_date>/ and produce these outputs:
   - out/playlist.csv: one row per selected track with columns: source, identifier, title, duration_sec, file_size_bytes, license, download_url, matched_keyword, matched_school, rank.
   - out/playlist.json: an array of the same selected tracks with the same fields as playlist.csv plus run_date.
   - out/cron.txt: a single crontab line that runs weekly on the weekday and time specified in input/preferences.yaml, invoking: python3 scripts/fetch_playlist.py from the repository root. It’s fine to use a relative path in the cron line. The line should redirect stdout/stderr to a log file under out/ (e.g., out/fetch.log).

Constraints and notes:
- Do not include hardcoded direct URLs in the request; discover resources via the official public APIs for the specified domains.
- Create any missing output directories.
- If fewer than max_items are found, still write all outputs with whatever items are available.
- The script performs a single run when executed; scheduling is represented by out/cron.txt and does not need to modify the host system.

Explicit paths:
- Inputs: input/preferences.yaml, input/schools.csv
- Outputs: out/audio/<run_date>/, out/playlist.csv, out/playlist.json, out/cron.txt
- Runner: scripts/fetch_playlist.py All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
